properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 31: Foreign Resident - Life Event Exception**

Australian purchased property, became foreign resident due to work overseas. Eligible for life event exception.

**Timeline:**
- 15 May 2015 to 1 Aug 2020: Main residence (Australian resident) - 5.21 years
- 1 Aug 2020: Became foreign resident (work overseas - life event)
- 1 Aug 2020 to 15 Sep 2024: Foreign resident period (4.12 years)
- Property not rented - kept available for use
- Total ownership: 9.33 years

**Key Rules:**
- Post-9 May 2017: Foreign residents generally not eligible for main residence exemption
- **EXCEPTION**: Life event (employment overseas) allows continued exemption
- Property must not be rented (income-producing)
- Must have been main residence before becoming foreign resident
- Absence from Australia < 6 years qualifies
- **NO CGT PAYABLE** - Life event exception applies

**Expected Result:**
- NO CGT PAYABLE - Foreign resident life event exception applies
- Total capital gain (before exemption): $666,325
- Full exemption under life event provisions

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-145 ITAA 1997 (Absence from Dwelling)
- TD 2019/20 (Foreign Resident Main Residence Exemption)
- Life event exception for employment overseas
- Property not used to produce income

additional_info.expected_outcome: NO CGT PAYABLE - Foreign resident life event exception applies (TD 2019/20)
